title: Lamyra echinocephala
intro: Lamyra echinocephala, also known as the Lamb's Ears or Woolly Mullein, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Lamyra echinocephala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lamyra echinocephala include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, soothe respiratory issues, and support skin health.
taxonomy: Lamyra echinocephala is classified within the family of Heteroceridae, commonly referred to as the variegated or spiny-headed beetles. This classification places Lamyra echinocephala within the order Coleoptera, which comprises beetles. The family Heteroceridae is further grouped under the superfamily Byrrhoidea, which contains a diverse range of beetles. The classification of Lamyra echinocephala reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the beetle family. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and phylogenetic relationships of Lamyra echinocephala.
